headlines.title: Trump says Iran has not agreed to a deal because ‘they’re strong, they’re proud’
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said Iranian leaders have not yet reached a deal with the U.S. to end the ongoing war because they’re “strong” and “proud.”

headlines.title: Mamdani makes ‘high-risk, high-reward’ move to reshape New York’s voice in Washington
headlines.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is seeking to put his stamp on the city’s congressional delegation with a trio of endorsements against incumbent lawmakers and their picks, a move that could further expand the power of his democratic socialist movement or thwart its progress — and dent his own image in the process.“This is high-risk, high reward,” said Rebecca Katz, a prominent Democratic strategist and ally whose firm worked with Mamdani during his 2025 run.

headlines.title: Send your questions for Steve Kornacki
headlines.description: NBC News’ chief data analyst Steve Kornacki will answer NBC News subscriber questions about the midterm elections and more in a live Q&A on Tuesday at 12 p.m. ET.

headlines.title: Trump on Pressure to End Iran War With Peace Deal
headlines.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to share key takeaways her exclusive interview with President Donald Trump about the war in Iran, its impact on the economy and his claims of voter fraud in California and the 2020 election. Kristen says, “I pressed him on his promise not to get the United States engaged in foreign wars. He said ‘This is not going to be a forever war.’ He insists it’s not a quagmire.”

headlines.title: Trump Says Iran Still Has '21%-22%' of Missile and Drone Capacity
headlines.description: Despite a ceasefire technically still in place, the United States and Iran continue to exchange attacks this weekend. President Donald Trump told Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker in an exclusive interview how many missiles his administration estimates that Iran has left. NBC’s Monica Alba reports for Sunday TODAY.

headlines.title: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner defends his past on campaign trail
headlines.description: At a rally with supporters, Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner spoke out to defend his past after appearing to address new allegations made against him by three women who spoke with the New York Times. Some voters said they are sticking with Platner despite the controversy. NBC News’ Monica Alba reports.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: ‘Maine had my back’: Graham Platner hits the campaign trail amid latest turmoil
headlines.description: Hundreds of supporters signaled they weren’t phased by the new allegations about Graham Platner’s past relationships, giving the Democratic Senate candidate a warm welcome on Friday.

headlines.title: Pentagon raised threat of Israeli spying on U.S. to highest level, sources say
headlines.description: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon is increasingly concerned about Israel ramping up its spying on the U.S., recently raising the counterintelligence threat level from America’s top ally in the Middle East to the highest level, according to two U.S. officials and one former U.S. official

headlines.title: Graham Platner supporter Ro Khanna says he should apologize to women who alleged disturbing behavior
headlines.description: BAR HARBOR, Maine — Rep. Ro Khanna of California on Friday said that Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner should apologize to the women who recently came forward describing disturbing behavior during their relationships with him

headlines.title: DOJ office says ‘multiple’ probes of California elections underway after Trump cries foul
headlines.description: A federal prosecutor indicated the investigations are focused on the Los Angeles area following President Donald Trump’s claims, without evidence, of cheating in the primaries.
